name: NI006 reduced ATTR-CM amyloid-load biomarkers in phase 1
summary: NI006 is direct human biomarker evidence for an antibody intended to remove existing cardiac transthyretin amyloid.
statement: In ATTR cardiomyopathy, NI006 was designed to remove cardiac ATTR through phagocytic immune cells and, at doses of at least 10 mg/kg, appeared to reduce imaging-based amyloid-load markers over 12 months.
population_or_model: Patients with wild-type or variant ATTR cardiomyopathy and chronic heart failure
time_horizon: Four monthly infusions plus open-label extension through 12 months of biomarker observation
quantitative_note: The phase 1 report states that cardiac tracer uptake and extracellular volume appeared reduced at doses of at least 10 mg/kg, with median NT-proBNP and troponin T levels also seeming to fall.
caveats: This was a small phase 1 trial designed primarily for safety and pharmacokinetics.
The evidence is biomarker-level and does not establish functional, hospitalization, or mortality benefit.
ATTR cardiomyopathy is an age-associated amyloid disease but not a general aging-proteostasis endpoint.
